questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the Brahmastra is the celestial weapon presided over by which deity?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is the divine missile presided over by Brahma, the creator.

questions.q: What kind of weapon is the Brahmastra described as in the epics and Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is described as a supremely destructive divine missile of immense power.

questions.q: How is the Brahmastra invoked by a warrior or sage in the texts?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is invoked by the recitation of a sacred mantra.

questions.q: Which quality is most often attributed to the Brahmastra in the epics?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is said to never miss its intended target once released.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which prince finally slays the demon king Ravana with the Brahmastra arrow?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Rama slays Ravana with the Brahmastra, a divine arrow charged with the power of Brahma.

questions.q: In which two major bodies of Hindu narrative literature is the Brahmastra most prominently described?
options: The Yoga Sutras and the Upanishads
The epics (Ramayana and Mahabharata) and the Puranas
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra appears prominently across the epics, the Ramayana and Mahabharata, and the Puranas.

questions.q: Who alone are said to be worthy to wield the Brahmastra in the epics?
options: Only the greatest warriors and sages
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is wielded only by the greatest warriors and sages who possess the discipline to command it.

questions.q: What scale of destruction is the Brahmastra said to be capable of in the texts?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra's terrible power could lay waste to entire regions, making its use a grave matter.

questions.q: The Brahmastra is named after Brahma, who holds which role in the Hindu Trimurti?
questions.explanation: Brahma, after whom the Brahmastra is named, is the creator within the Trimurti.

questions.q: What was demanded of any warrior before releasing the Brahmastra, according to the lore?
questions.explanation: Releasing the Brahmastra demanded the highest discipline and restraint from its wielder.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which sage instructs Rama in the Aditya Hridayam and assists him before his final battle with Ravana?
questions.explanation: The sage Agastya teaches Rama the Aditya Hridayam hymn and encourages him before his duel with Ravana.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the Brahmastra is associated with which Pandava archer renowned for his celestial weapons?
questions.explanation: Arjuna, master of celestial weapons, possessed knowledge of the Brahmastra in the Mahabharata.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, who was the teacher that imparted the Brahmastra to both Arjuna and Ashwatthama?
questions.explanation: The guru Drona taught the Brahmastra to his pupils, including Arjuna and his own son Ashwatthama.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, when both Arjuna and Ashwatthama release the Brahmastra, who intervenes to prevent universal catastrophe?
questions.explanation: The sages Narada and Vyasa intervene between the two clashing weapons to avert cosmic destruction.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, who is said to lack the knowledge to withdraw the Brahmastra once it has been launched?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama could invoke the Brahmastra but had not learned how to recall it once released.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Ashwatthama directs the Brahmastra toward the womb of which princess, threatening her unborn child?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama directs the Brahmastra at the womb of Uttara, seeking to end the Pandava lineage.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, who saves Uttara's unborn child Parikshit from the Brahmastra's destructive power?
questions.explanation: Krishna protects the unborn Parikshit from the Brahmastra, ensuring the survival of the dynasty.

questions.q: What is the general Sanskrit term for divine weapons such as the Brahmastra that are invoked by mantra?
questions.explanation: Weapons summoned and presided over by deities through mantra are called astras, of which the Brahmastra is foremost.

questions.q: In the Sundara Kanda of the Ramayana, which warrior binds Hanuman by invoking the Brahmastra in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Ravana's son Indrajit binds Hanuman with the Brahmastra when Hanuman enters Lanka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, how does Hanuman respond when bound by the Brahmastra invoked by Indrajit?
options: He breaks it instantly and flees
He calls upon Rama to free him
He honours it and allows himself to remain captured
questions.explanation: Out of reverence for Brahma's weapon, Hanuman honours the Brahmastra and permits himself to be taken before Ravana.

questions.q: Why is the Brahmastra never to be used against an unworthy or lesser foe, according to the epics?
options: Its devastation is far too great for a minor adversary
It can only be used at night
It must be used only in water
questions.explanation: Because its devastation is so vast, the Brahmastra must never be unleashed upon a lesser or undeserving foe.

questions.q: An even more powerful weapon mentioned alongside the Brahmastra in the texts is which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Brahmashira astra is described as a still more fearsome escalation of the Brahmastra.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, with what kind of arrow is Rama said to finally strike down Ravana in their duel?
options: A divine arrow charged with the power of Brahma
questions.explanation: Rama slays Ravana with a great arrow charged with the irresistible power of Brahma, the Brahmastra.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, after the sages halt the duel, what does Arjuna do with his Brahmastra that Ashwatthama cannot?
options: He hurls it at the sky
He destroys it with the Vajra
He withdraws and recalls the weapon safely
questions.explanation: Arjuna, having learned the full discipline of the weapon, is able to withdraw his Brahmastra without harm.

questions.q: As a consequence of misusing the Brahmastra against Uttara's child, what curse does Krishna lay upon Ashwatthama in the Mahabharata?
options: To wander for ages, afflicted and unable to die
questions.explanation: Krishna curses Ashwatthama to wander the earth for a vast span of time, suffering and unable to find death.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, what is said to follow in a land where the Brahmastra is unleashed, reflecting its terrible aftermath?
questions.explanation: The texts describe that where the Brahmastra strikes, rains fail and the land turns barren for a long period.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Ravana's son Indrajit (Meghanada) was himself a master of the Brahmastra; by whose hand is he ultimately slain in the war?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, a wielder of the Brahmastra, is finally slain by Rama's brother Lakshmana in the war at Lanka.

questions.q: Which quality distinguishes the Brahmastra from ordinary astras in the lore of celestial weapons?
options: It is the weakest of all astras
It can be wielded without training
It causes no harm to anyone
It is among the most supreme and irresistible of all astras
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra ranks among the most supreme and irresistible of all celestial weapons in Hindu lore.

questions.q: According to the texts, what spiritual merit was generally required to obtain and command the Brahmastra?
options: Tapas (austerity) and instruction from a worthy teacher
questions.explanation: Mastery of the Brahmastra required tapas and proper instruction from a qualified guru or sage.

questions.q: The Brahmastra is the weapon of Brahma, while a comparably supreme astra is attributed to Shiva under which name?
questions.explanation: Just as the Brahmastra belongs to Brahma, the Pashupatastra is the supreme weapon attributed to Shiva.
